article_meta.title: Defense Coast Guard denies report it won't classify swastikas, nooses as hate symbols by Filip Timotija 11/20/25 03:23 P

reasoning: The summary maintains neutral language, attributes charged statements to sources, and avoids adopting the article’s sensational phrasing. It does, however, under-represent some perspectives and simplifies the multi-sided context.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (denied, reported, refuted, confirmed) and avoids loaded phrasing.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All contested claims are attributed to the Coast Guard, DHS, or the Washington Post.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments asserted by the model; evaluative language appears only in attributed quotes.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Does not replicate or amplify the article’s harsher language; keeps a measured tone.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks by the model; hostile language appears only as quoted attributions.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No speculation about motives or mental states.
custom_reasoning.slant: Slight emphasis on official denials over the details of the Post’s reporting and external criticism.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits Rep. Rick Larsen’s perspective and some procedural details (e.g., 45-day reporting window) present in the article.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Adjectives are either factual or quoted; none used by the model to editorialize.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically neutral terms throughout; charged terms only within quotes and attributed.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balance mirrors the source; no extra negativity introduced.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats media and government sources similarly; no deference beyond summarizing their statements.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Clearly more neutral than the source by avoiding loaded phrases and keeping attributions explicit.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Sources_represented lists only The Hill and omits Coast Guard, DHS, Washington Post, and Rep. Larsen, underrepresenting perspectives.
